stem: An engineer takes 30 measurements of fuel level using a fuel gauge. The measurements have a mean of 92.6 % and a standard deviation of 8.9 %. However, calibration tests reveal that a fuel gauge systematically overestimates the true value by between 3.9% and 9.7%. Estimate the true fuel level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 92.6 %. After correcting for the 6.8% bias: ≈ 86.7 %. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (3.9%-9.7%) and sampling variability: [81.2, 92.3]. Point estimate: 86.7 %.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
